body_txt: In Real Life , people can turn pale when ill or shocked. In animation, they can go grayscale or pure white. These events are usually played for comedy and characters once grayscale can be unresponsive or completely frozen in place. See also Blue with Shock , an artistically different and most of the time less extreme version, common with Blank White Eyes . Compare Deliberately Monochrome . Common side effect of a Corner of Woe . Not to be confused with colors looking wrong on something due to artistic oversight. See Death Is Gray , Gray Is Useless , and No Power, No Color for instances of this occurring for more dire reasons.
